title: sclerolaena clelandii
intro: Sclerolaena clelandii, also known as Cleland's bitter saltbush, is a plant species native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, including being used to treat fever, rheumatic pain, and as an antiseptic. The plant's leaves and stems have been used in herbal preparations to relieve pain, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. In addition, the plant's antiseptic properties have been used to treat skin conditions and as a natural antimicrobial agent in herbal remedies.
